goal: Read through the codebase directory structure and look for patterns indicating how the system adapts to user-specific communication styles.

plan_steps.description: List all files in the codebase directory structure.
acceptance_criteria: A comprehensive list of all files in the codebase is generated.

plan_steps.description: Search for files containing keywords related to communication styles, such as 'style', 'user-specific', and 'adaptation'.
acceptance_criteria: A list of files containing the specified keywords is generated.

plan_steps.description: Read and analyze the content of identified files to identify patterns in how the system adapts to user-specific communication styles.
acceptance_criteria: Key patterns or code snippets indicating adaptation to user-specific communication styles are extracted and documented.

plan_steps.description: Summarize the findings from the analysis into a structured report.
acceptance_criteria: A summary report detailing identified patterns is created and saved in a designated location.

spoken_summary: I ran into a wall pretty quick — I tried to search the codebase but that tool isn't available to me, so I couldn't dig into the directory structure or find patterns about how the system adapts to different communication styles. Want to point me at a specific folder I can read through instead?
